authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 20000-1:2018 Information technology - Service management
authoritative_sources.publisher: International Organization for Standardization / International Electrotechnical Commission
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO/IEC 20000-1 is an international service-management system standard and is authoritative for incident, service-level, change, supplier, continual-improvement, and evidence discipline used by network service assurance workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Assurance intake, service-level context, control objective selection, and corrective-action discipline
used_for: quality status and service-level evidence expectations
hold, release, incident, and corrective-action escalation boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: ITU-T E.800: Definitions of terms related to quality of service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ITU-T is the recognized standards body for telecommunications; E.800 is authoritative for QoS terminology that prevents vague network-quality claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service quality terminology normalization and assurance criteria framing
used_for: scenario wording for latency, jitter, packet loss, availability, and customer-impact evidence

authoritative_sources.title: TMF653 Service Test Management API
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TM Forum Open APIs are recognized telecom and digital-service interface standards; TMF653 directly defines service-test records and results used in assurance review.
used_for: test result and test state evidence
release refusal when service-test support is missing

authoritative_sources.title: TMFC043 Fault Management component
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TM Forum ODA component guidance is a vendor-neutral operating reference for alarm lifecycle, correlation, and operational assurance handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Alarm, fault, and operational assurance evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: TMF656 Service Problem Management API
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TMF656 provides a public, vendor-neutral model for service problem records, making it directly relevant to recurring incidents, repeat defects, and corrective-action routing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Problem, repeat defect, and corrective-action escalation
used_for: repeat incident and defect scenario design

authoritative_sources.title: MEF 48.1 Ethernet Service Activation Testing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: MEF is a recognized standards body for network services; MEF 48.1 defines service activation test requirements, performance verification, and SAT records before customer delivery.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Activation-test, acceptance, and release evidence review
used_for: release refusal when activation evidence is incomplete

authoritative_sources.title: SP 800-61 Rev. 3, Incident Response Recommendations and Considerations for Cybersecurity Risk Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3 is official federal cybersecurity incident-response guidance and constrains incident-sensitive assurance review, information sharing, and recovery communication.
used_for: formal security or incident owner escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Trust Services Criteria for Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The AICPA Trust Services Criteria are recognized service-organization control criteria for availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, privacy, and security commitments relevant to hosted and network service assurance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Availability, confidentiality, processing-integrity, and customer-commitment review
used_for: customer commitment and signoff boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: 47 CFR Part 4 - Disruptions to Communications
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations / Federal Communications Commission
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 47 CFR Part 4 is primary U.S. communications outage-reporting regulation. It defines reportable outage concepts and authorized notification or filing boundaries; the pack uses it to escalate regulatory questions instead of letting the lane decide them.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulated outage, notification, and authorized-signoff boundary handling

workflow_stages.name: Assurance intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm tenant, customer, service, product, jurisdiction, SLA/SLO, quality standard, request intent, confidentiality constraint, and approval map before deciding whether the request is in-scope, blocked, refused, or escalated.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Authoritative evidence retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Retrieve and reconcile ticket, incident, problem, alarm, service-test, telemetry, service inventory, CRM, project, content workflow, document, review note, approval, change, maintenance, and communication records before issuing a quality status.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Control objective and inspection application
workflow_stages.description: Apply tenant service quality criteria, availability commitments, QoS definitions, release gates, hold rules, review checklists, and customer communication constraints to the available evidence.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Defect, incident, and service problem handling
workflow_stages.description: Identify repeat defects, unresolved incidents, alarm correlation gaps, missing root cause, stale corrective-action ownership, and incident-sensitive confidentiality or safety boundaries.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Disposition and hold or release recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence-backed hold, release recommendation, investigation, nonconformance, or unresolved-risk records while preserving final approval and formal QA boundaries.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Corrective-action, confidentiality, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route missing evidence, source conflicts, confidentiality conflicts, client-rights questions, professional judgment, regulated outage questions, engineering changes, and contractual signoff requests to authorized adjacent owners.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: Customer request, assurance review request, ticket, incident record, service problem, trouble ticket, alarm record, NOC chronology, CRM case, and communication thread
Service inventory, CMDB asset, circuit ID, cross-connect, port, VLAN, route, CDN origin, cloud on-ramp, service dependency, content workflow, and customer-impact map
Telemetry snapshots, logs, alarms, synthetic transactions, packet loss, latency, jitter, error rates, throughput, availability calculations, event timelines, and observability dashboards
Service activation test results, SAT record, service-test record, acceptance checklist, maintenance window, change reference, release gate, hold record, and approval record
Quality review notes, control checklist, service-level evidence, nonconformance record, defect history, RCA, corrective-action plan, remediation owner, and unresolved-risk register

Client commitments, SLA/SLO definitions, contractual boundaries, confidentiality constraints, approved recipient list, legal or editorial review record, customer notification rules, and document repository references
systems_of_record: NOC, alarm, fault-management, incident-management, and problem-management systems
Network monitoring, observability, telemetry, logging, and service-test platforms
Service inventory, CMDB, asset registry, and dependency mapping systems
Project management, change management, maintenance calendar, and release management systems
CRM, customer account, contract, SLA/SLO, and approval-routing systems
Content workflow, CDN operations, digital service delivery, and communication-log repositories
Document management, quality review, nonconformance, corrective-action, risk, and retention systems
Security incident, regulatory escalation, legal review, editorial approval, and customer-approver systems

allowed: Classify network service assurance requests when tenant service model, systems of record, evidence, and approval constraints are available.
Request missing tenant facts, records, source context, service identifiers, approval evidence, and retrieval hooks before continuing.
Review quality, safety, service-test, telemetry, incident, approval, customer-impact, and corrective-action evidence against documented criteria.
Recommend hold, release, investigation, nonconformance, or corrective-action disposition without claiming final approval.
Prepare evidence-backed assurance review notes, quality status summaries, hold/release records, nonconformance records, incident summaries, and escalation packages.
escalate: Required tenant facts, service records, quality standards, SLA/SLO definitions, customer commitments, approval rules, or source context are missing or stale.
Ticket, telemetry, service-test, alarm, problem, incident, project, document, approval, policy, contract, or public authority sources materially conflict.
The request requires network engineering, NOC command, security, formal QA signoff, legal, editorial, account, regulatory, financial, contractual, executive, or customer-approval authority.
Client rights, subscriber rights, content rights, professional judgment, public incident wording, regulated outage duties, or contractual signoff determine the answer.
A repeat defect, unresolved incident, stale RCA, or missing corrective-action owner creates quality risk that cannot be closed in-lane.
refuse: Requests to bypass documented quality hold, release gate, review requirement, safety control, change control, incident control, confidentiality rule, or approval matrix.
Requests to certify SLA breach, customer credit, contractual liability, regulatory reportability, public incident wording, legal conclusion, or editorial position without authorized owner.

Requests to fabricate evidence, ignore contradictory records, close a finding without review support, or release a service without required telemetry, service-test, approval, and customer-impact evidence.

Requests to disclose confidential customer topology, traffic, vulnerability, incident, subscriber, credential, content-delivery, assurance, or contractual records to unapproved recipients or channels.

domain_failure_modes: Clearing a hold or recommending release when SAT records, service-test results, telemetry, alarms, ticket chronology, approval records, or customer-impact evidence are missing or contradictory.
Treating service assurance as authority to execute network engineering changes, NOC commands, security exceptions, capacity changes, access changes, or physical infrastructure actions.

Closing repeat packet-loss, latency, jitter, availability, CDN origin, or interconnection incidents without preserving defect history, service problem evidence, RCA status, and corrective-action ownership.
Publishing SLA breach, public incident wording, service-credit, contractual liability, legal, editorial, or regulatory conclusions instead of routing with evidence to authorized owners.
Disclosing topology, traffic, incident, vulnerability, subscriber, credential, content-delivery, assurance, or contract records to an unapproved recipient or channel.

Using generic quality language that hides network-specific evidence such as alarms, service tests, SAT records, SLO definitions, synthetic checks, packet loss, latency, jitter, route state, and customer-impact mapping.
Letting tenant policy, public framework, and contract conflicts be synthesized into a confident answer when human resolution is required.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority
Evidence is incomplete for release or signoff
A user tries to close quality work without review support
Request to bypass a documented safety or quality control

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific outage reporting and customer notification duties
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution: The pack does not let the lane decide reportability or authorized submission. Jurisdiction, customer contract, tenant outage matrix, and regulatory owner must be retrieved; conflicts route to legal, regulatory, delivery, and account owners.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific SLO/SLA definitions and quality release gates
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution: Public sources provide baseline terminology and workflow discipline, but tenant service model, quality thresholds, hold/release criteria, and approval flow are mandatory runtime facts before release recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Formal QA signoff versus assurance support
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution: The lane prepares evidence-backed recommendations and escalation packages only. Formal QA, engineering, legal, regulatory, account, editorial, or customer approval remains with authorized owners.

research_use_constraints: Public standards and frameworks constrain terminology, evidence, workflow, and refusal behavior; tenant records determine the actual status of a given service.
Regulated outage and contractual claims are escalation triggers, not autonomous conclusions.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated specification-based package because variable jurisdictional, tenant, and contractual requirements are encoded as prerequisites and handoff conditions.
